user_query:
I'm a Swedish interior designer auditing a small supplier catalog and polishing some outreach. Please automate an inspection and produce concise, client-ready comms using the provided inputs.

Inputs (all in the workspace):
- input/products.csv
- input/suppliers.json
- input/draft_messages.md

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Build and RUN a small command-line script (any language) that reads input/products.csv and input/suppliers.json and writes all of the following files under output/:
   - output/summary_by_material.csv with columns exactly: material,active_product_count,avg_price_sek,min_price_sek,max_price_sek,fsc_certified_count,fsc_certified_share. Rules:
     • Treat products with discontinued=yes as inactive; exclude them from counts/stats. 
     • avg_price_sek must be the arithmetic mean of price_sek for active products of that material, rounded to the nearest whole SEK.
     • fsc_certified_share is the share of active products with certification=="FSC" within each material, expressed as a decimal rounded to 2 decimals (e.g., 0.40).
   - output/supplier_overview.csv with columns exactly: supplier_id,supplier_name,active_product_count,total_stock_qty,mean_lead_time_days. Rules:
     • Use supplier names and lead times from input/suppliers.json by matching supplier_id.
     • active_product_count and total_stock_qty include only active (not discontinued) products.
     • mean_lead_time_days is the arithmetic mean of lead_time_days across the supplier’s records (from suppliers.json), rounded to the nearest whole day.
   - output/flagged_products.csv listing data issues with columns exactly: product_id,name,issue. Include a row for each active or inactive product that has at least one of:
     • non_positive_price (price_sek <= 0)
     • invalid_dimensions (dimensions_cm not in LxWxH with three positive numbers)
     • missing_or_unknown_certification (certification not equal to "FSC" or "None")
     If multiple issues apply, create one row per issue for that product.

   The script must be runnable locally and reproducible; do not hardcode expected values. Save a simple run log to output/run_log.txt that includes: the exact command you executed to generate the reports, the working directory, and a single line in the form "flagged_products_count: N" with N equal to the number of rows in output/flagged_products.csv.

2) Rewrite the notes in input/draft_messages.md into one consolidated file output/rewritten_messages.md:
   - Produce a polished version of each draft (keep the same order and headings), each under 120 words, in a clear, professional tone appropriate for Swedish design clients and suppliers.
   - Preserve factual details (product names, dates, supplier IDs) from the drafts.
   - For the [SUPPLIER] message, include at least one numeric figure taken directly from output/summary_by_material.csv (e.g., an average price or FSC share with its material name). Do not invent numbers.
   - For the [CLIENT] message, retain the meeting date.
   - For the [SOCIAL] message, keep it to 1–2 sentences with a simple call to action.

Notes:
- Use only the provided local files; no internet fetching.
- I will validate by inspecting your outputs and cross-checking them against the inputs and your run log.

Expected output paths: scripts/ (your script), output/summary_by_material.csv, output/supplier_overview.csv, output/flagged_products.csv, output/rewritten_messages.md, output/run_log.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
